content.summary:
This project targets the aggressive removal of ~2,000 lines of dead pipeline/flow infrastructure across 43 files, simplification of the system view to visualize only real services, and addition of lightweight project awareness to the features board. Research confirms the system view is built entirely on `@xyflow/react` [2] with a three-lane hardcoded topology [3], data assembled from engine status polling and WebSocket events [4][7]. The features board already has complete project-linking infrastructure (`projectSlug`, `milestoneSlug`, `phaseSlug`) [12][20] and a ready-made `useProjectFeatures()` hook [15][23]. Pipeline deletion is safe from a CI/coverage perspective: pipeline tests are fully self-contained [26][27], coverage thresholds exclude routes [31], and no remote coverage gate is enforced [34]. The sole regression guard for the system view is a single Playwright E2E smoke test [29][30] that must be updatedâ€”not deletedâ€”if route or test-ID changes occur.

---
content.fullReport:
# Research Report: Pipeline Cleanup & System View Simplification

## Summary

This project targets the aggressive removal of ~2,000 lines of dead pipeline/flow infrastructure across 43 files, simplification of the system view to visualize only real services, and addition of lightweight project awareness to the features board. Research confirms the system view is built entirely on `@xyflow/react` [2] with a three-lane hardcoded topology [3], data assembled from engine status polling and WebSocket events [4][7]. The features board already has complete project-linking infrastructure (`projectSlug`, `milestoneSlug`, `phaseSlug`) [12][20] and a ready-made `useProjectFeatures()` hook [15][23]. Pipeline deletion is safe from a CI/coverage perspective: pipeline tests are fully self-contained [26][27], coverage thresholds exclude routes [31], and no remote coverage gate is enforced [34]. The sole regression guard for the system view is a single Playwright E2E smoke test [29][30] that must be updatedâ€”not deletedâ€”if route or test-ID changes occur.

---

## Codebase Findings

### System View Architecture

The system view is routed through `AnalyticsView` â†’ `FlowGraphView` â†’ `FlowGraphCanvas` [1]. The sole rendering library is **`@xyflow/react` (XYFlow v12.10.0)**â€”no D3 or Cytoscape dependencies exist [2]:

```tsx
// FILE: apps/ui/src/components/views/flow-graph/flow-graph-canvas.tsx:9-21
import {
  ReactFlow, Background, BackgroundVariant, Controls,
  useNodesState, useEdgesState, type Node, type Edge
} from '@xyflow/react';
import '@xyflow/react/dist/style.css';
```

The topology is a **three-lane static layout** defined in `constants.ts` [3]:
- **Lane 1 (y=50):** Signal Sources â†’ Triage â†’ Decomposition â†’ Launch
- **Lane 2 (y=280):** Lead Engineer Rules â†” Auto-Mode â†’ Agent Execution â†’ Git â†’ PR Feedback
- **Lane 3 (y=510):** Backlog â†’ In Progress â†’ Review â†’ Done

Node IDs are statically defined [3]:

```ts
// FILE: apps/ui/src/components/views/flow-graph/constants.ts
export const NODE_IDS = {
  signalSources: 'engine-signal-sources', triage: 'engine-triage',
  decomposition: 'engine-decomposition', launch: 'engine-launch',
  leadEngineerRules: 'engine-lead-engineer-rules', autoMode: 'engine-auto-mode',
  agentExecution: 'engine-agent-execution', gitWorkflow: 'engine-git-workflow',
  prFeedback: 'engine-pr-feedback', contentPipeline: 'engine-content-pipeline',
  reflection: 'engine-reflection', github: 'integration-github',
  discord: 'integration-discord'
} as const;
```

### Data Assembly & Node Taxonomy

The `use-flow-graph-data.ts` hook assembles nodes and edges from four data sources: engine service status (`/api/engine/status`), WebSocket pipeline events, integration status, and running agents [4]. Polling intervals are 10 seconds for engine status and 30 seconds for integration status [7].

Five distinct node data models exist: `EngineServiceNodeData`, `IntegrationNodeData`, `PipelineStageNodeData`, `FeatureNodeData`, and `AgentNodeData` [10]. Static nodes coexist with dynamic feature/agent nodes spawned conditionally [5]:

```ts
// FILE: apps/ui/src/components/views/flow-graph/hooks/use-flow-graph-data.ts:407-415
const activeFeatures = useMemo(
  () => features.filter((f) => {
    const s = f.status as string;
    return s === 'in_progress' || s === 'review';
  }),
  [features]
);
```

The only existing visibility filter is GTM-gated: when `engineStatus.gtmEnabled === false`, the `content-pipeline` node and its edges are pruned [6]:

```ts
// FILE: apps/ui/src/components/views/flow-graph/hooks/use-flow-graph-data.ts:422-425
const services = gtmEnabled
  ? ENGINE_SERVICES
  : ENGINE_SERVICES.filter((s) => s.serviceId !== 'content-pipeline');
```

Five edge types drive distinct SVG animations: `delegation`, `workflow`, `integration`, `pipeline`, and `flow-edge` [8].

### Pipeline Phaseâ€“Service Mapping (Dead Code Target)

A static constant maps 9 pipeline phases to responsible engine services, driving pulsing highlights on the graph [9]:

```ts
// FILE: apps/ui/src/components/views/flow-graph/constants.ts:383-396
export const PIPELINE_PHASE_TO_SERVICE: Record<
  PipelinePhase, { ops: EngineServiceId; gtm: EngineServiceId }
> = {
  TRIAGE:  { ops: 'triage', gtm: 'triage' },
  EXECUTE: { ops: 'agent-execution', gtm: 'content-pipeline' },
  PUBLISH: { ops: 'git-workflow', gtm: 'content-pipeline' },
  // ...
};
```

This constant and the `PipelineStageNodeData` type [10] are prime deletion candidatesâ€”they represent the virtual pipeline overlay on top of real services.

### Features Board & Project Awareness

The features board is already project-scoped at the data-fetch layer. `useFeatures(currentProject?.path)` loads features keyed on `projectPath` [13], and `useBoardFeatures` enforces per-project event isolation [14][22]:

```typescript
// FILE: apps/ui/src/components/views/board-view/hooks/use-board-features.ts
export function useBoardFeatures({ currentProject }: UseBoardFeaturesProps) {
  const { data: features = [] } = useFeatures(currentProject?.path);
  useEffect(() => {
    const unsubscribe = api.autoMode.onEvent((event) => {
      if (eventProjectPath && eventProjectPath !== projectPath) return;
    });
    return unsubscribe;
  }, [currentProject?.path]);
}
```

Features link to projects via three slug fields [12][20]:

```typescript
// FILE: libs/types/src/feature.ts:172-175
projectSlug?: string;
milestoneSlug?: string;
phaseSlug?: string;
isEpic?: boolean;
epicId?: string;
epicColor?: string;
```

The `Project` type uses `slug` as its unique key and holds `milestones[]` â†’ `phases[]` hierarchy [11][21]. A dedicated `useProjectFeatures(projectSlug)` hook already exists and is query-ready [15][23]:

```typescript
// FILE: apps/ui/src/components/views/projects-view/hooks/use-project-features.ts
export function useProjectFeatures(projectSlug: string | null) {
  const projectPath = useAppStore((s) => s.currentProject?.path) ?? '';
  return useQuery({
    queryKey: ['project-features', projectPath, projectSlug],
    queryFn: async () => await api.lifecycle.getProjectFeatures(projectPath, projectSlug!),
    enabled: !!projectPath && !!projectSlug,
  });
}
```

Board columns are purely status-derived (`ColumnId = Feature['status']`) with no project filtering at the column level [19][25]. The `AppState` Zustand store exposes `currentProject` globally [18][24].

Server-side, `orchestrateProjectFeatures()` bridges planâ†’board by iterating milestonesâ†’phases and creating epics/features with backfilled slug refs [16]. A three-way `projectSlug` resolution strategy exists: direct field, `epicId` chain, or `milestoneSlug` lookup [17].

### Testing & Safe Deletion Constraints

**Tests to DELETE (pipeline-coupled):**

| File | Lines | Reason |
|------|-------|--------|
| `apps/server/tests/unit/routes/pipeline.test.ts` | ~500 | Imports six pipeline route handlers and `PipelineService` [26] |
| `apps/server/tests/unit/services/pipeline-service.test.ts` | ~860 | Directly instantiates `PipelineService` with mocked `secure-fs` [27] |

**Tests to PRESERVE (decoupled):**

| File | Reason |
|------|--------|
| `apps/server/tests/knowledge-flow-pipeline.test.ts` | Tests knowledge domain, not pipeline infra [28] |
| `apps/server/tests/unit/routes/board.test.ts` | Tests features route (`computeWipSaturation`) [33] |
| `apps/ui/tests/features/feature-manual-review-flow.spec.ts` | Validates kanban board workflow [35] |
| `libs/flows/tests/unit/maintenance-flow.test.ts` | Tests `createMaintenanceFlow()` with LangGraph mocks [36] |

**Test to UPDATE:**

The Playwright E2E smoke test at `apps/ui/tests/views/flow-graph-smoke.spec.ts` [29][30] is the **sole regression guard** for the system view:

```typescript
// FILE: apps/ui/tests/views/flow-graph-smoke.spec.ts:19-43
test('should render analytics view with flow graph canvas', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto(`${UI_BASE_URL}/analytics`);
  const flowGraphView = page.getByTestId('flow-graph-view');
  await expect(flowGraphView).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 });
  const flowGraphCanvas = page.getByTestId('flow-graph-canvas');
  await expect(flowGraphCanvas).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 });
  const nodes = page.locator('.react-flow__node');
  await expect(nodes.first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 });
});
```

Zero unit tests exist for `system-view.tsx`, `board-view.tsx`, or the flow graph component tree [30].

**Coverage safety:** Routes are excluded from coverage thresholds [31], thresholds are set at lines: 60%, functions: 75%, branches: 55%, statements: 60% [31], and the remote codecov upload is commented out [34]. Pipeline deletion will not trigger threshold failures.

---

## Relevant Patterns & Integration Points

### Critical Dependency Chain
1. **`use-flow-graph-data.ts`** â†’ consumes `ENGINE_SERVICES` and `PIPELINE_PHASE_TO_SERVICE` from `constants.ts` [4][9]
2. **`use-pipeline-tracker.ts`** â†’ feeds WebSocket events into graph node highlighting [4][7]
3. **`PipelineStageNodeData`** type in `types.ts` [10] â†’ used by pipeline-stage nodes in the assembly logic
4. **`constants.ts:PIPELINE_PHASE_TO_SERVICE`** [9] â†’ maps phases to services for pulsing animations

### Simplification Path
The system view can be reduced to show only **engine-service** and **integration** node types by:
- Removing `PipelineStageNodeData` from types [10]
- Removing `PIPELINE_PHASE_TO_SERVICE` from constants [9]
- Stripping pipeline-stage node creation from `use-flow-graph-data.ts` [4]
- Eliminating Lane 3 (pipeline kanban stages) from the static layout [3]
- Removing the `pipeline` edge type from the edge registry [8]

### Board Enhancement Path
Adding project awareness requires minimal new code because:
- `currentProject` is globally available in Zustand [18][24]
- `useProjectFeatures()` hook is already implemented [15][23]
- Featureâ†’Project linking fields exist and are populated [12][17]
- Board columns are status-derived, requiring no structural changes [19]

---

## External Research

No external sources were required. All findings are derived from codebase analysis across 36 source files spanning the UI application (`apps/ui/`), server (`apps/server/`), shared types (`libs/types/`), and CI configuration (`.github/workflows/`).

---

## Recommended Approach

### Phase 1: Pipeline Infrastructure Deletion (~1,360 lines of test code + production routes)
1. **Delete** `apps/server/tests/unit/routes/pipeline.test.ts` (~500 lines) [26]
2. **Delete** `apps/server/tests/unit/services/pipeline-service.test.ts` (~860 lines) [27]
3. **Delete** all pipeline route handlers imported in the test file (`get-config`, `save-config`, `add-step`, `update-step`, `delete-step`, `reorder-steps`) [26]
4. **Delete** `PipelineService` [27]
5. **Verify** `knowledge-flow-pipeline.test.ts` remains untouched (different domain) [28][32]

### Phase 2: System View Simplification
1. **Remove** `PIPELINE_PHASE_TO_SERVICE` from `constants.ts` [9]
2. **Remove** `PipelineStageNodeData` from `types.ts` [10]
3. **Remove** Lane 3 static positions from `constants.ts` [3]
4. **Strip** pipeline-stage and `pipeline` edge creation from `use-flow-graph-data.ts` [4][6]
5. **Remove** the `pipeline` edge type from the edge registry [8]
6. **Preserve** the GTM visibility filter pattern as a model for future service toggling [6]
7. **Update** the E2E smoke test to match the simplified node set [29]â€”this is the **only** regression guard [30]

### Phase 3: Features Board Project Awareness
1. **Surface** `currentProject` metadata (title, slug) in the board header using existing Zustand state [18][24]
2. **Leverage** existing `useProjectFeatures(projectSlug)` hook [15][23] to display milestone/phase context on board cards
3. **Use** the existing `projectSlug`/`milestoneSlug`/`phaseSlug` fields on features [12][20]â€”no schema changes needed
4. **Verify** `BoardHeader` component can surface project metadata or add a lightweight display component

### Execution Order
Phases 1â†’2 should be sequential (delete infrastructure first, then simplify the view that consumed it). Phase 3 is independent and can proceed in parallel with Phase 2.

---

## Open Questions & Risks

| # | Question / Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|----------------|----------|------------|
| 1 | **`getServiceStatus()` internals unknown**â€”simplification must preserve the status-resolution logic for remaining engine-service nodes | Medium | Read `use-flow-graph-data.ts` fully before modifying; function likely computes `healthy`/`degraded`/`offline` from `/api/engine/status` response |
| 2 | **`use-pipeline-tracker.ts` EVENT_STAGE_MAP not fully mapped**â€”unclear which WebSocket events will become orphan subscriptions after cleanup | Medium | Audit all `addEventListener`/`onEvent` calls in the tracker; remove subscriptions for deleted pipeline phases |
| 3 | **Single E2E test as sole regression guard** [29][30]â€”no unit tests exist for any system view component | High | Run the smoke test after every incremental change; consider adding a lightweight unit test for `use-flow-graph-data.ts` post-simplification |
| 4 | **`BoardHeader` project metadata display unverified**â€”unclear if a header component exists or needs creation | Low | Inspect `board-view` component tree; if absent, a small `<ProjectBadge>` component using `currentProject` from store [18] is trivial |
| 5 | **43 files claimed for deletion, but Architecture & Dead Code research returned no findings**â€”the full file manifest is unverified | High | Generate a complete deletion manifest via `grep -r` for `PipelineService`, `pipeline-config`, and pipeline route imports before executing Phase 1 |
| 6 | **No snapshot files exist** [30]â€”confirmed zero `.snap` regeneration burden, but also means no structural regression safety net beyond the E2E test | Low | Acceptable; snapshot tests for a visualization layer would be brittle anyway |

---

## Citations
